pattern: Each new bit shifts the running value left and adds the bit, but you only ever need that value modulo five, so the remainder updates as (r*2 + bit) mod 5 at every step.
hints: The prefix value doubles and gains the new bit each step; divisibility by five depends only on the value's remainder modulo five, not the full number.
Carry a running remainder r and update it with r = (r * 2 + nums[i]) mod 5 as you read each bit — the number itself never has to be built.
After updating r at index i, append true when r equals zero and false otherwise to your answer array.

edge_inputs.description: leading zero is divisible by five
five then ten in binary, both divisible

stress.description: long random bit array to exercise the running remainder
